summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Probability distribution'?

"Probability distribution" means: A function that assigns probabilities to every possible value of a random variable, with all probabilities summing to 1. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Uniform distribution'?

"Uniform distribution" means: A distribution where every outcome in the sample space has equal probability. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Expected value E(X)'?

"Expected value E(X)" means: The long-run average value of a random variable, calculated as the weighted mean of all possible values: E(X) = Σ[x·P(x)]. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standard deviation of a random variable'?

"Standard deviation of a random variable" means: The square root of variance, measuring the typical deviation of a random variable from its expected value: σ = √Var(X). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Cumulative distribution function (CDF)'?

"Cumulative distribution function (CDF)" means: F(x) = P(X ≤ x); gives the probability that a random variable takes a value less than or equal to x. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which term is described by: "The long-run average value of a random variable, calculated as the weighted mean of all possible values: E(X) = Σ[x·P(x)]"?

This description refers to "Expected value E(X)".
